steps.title: What the claim requires
steps.content: The text says Hedda seeks to influence another person's fate. The correct quotation must show her actively wanting to shape or control someone else's future — not her own uncertainty, not a rhetorical question, not commentary on character.

steps.title: Eliminate three, confirm one
steps.content:
• "Then what in heaven's name would you have me do with myself?": Hedda expresses confusion about her own life, not a desire to control anyone else's. ✗
  • "I want for once in my life to have power to mould a human destiny.": Explicitly states she wants power over another person's fate. This is exactly what the claim describes. ✓
  • "Then I, poor creature, have no sort of power over you?": A question implying she lacks power, not an expression of wanting to influence another's fate. ✗
  • "Faithful to your principles, now and for ever! Ah, that is how a man should be!": Commentary on someone else's character, not a desire to shape their destiny. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
